biomarkers.name: White Blood Cell Count
biomarkers.description: Total number of white blood cells. Elevated in infection or inflammation; low may indicate immune issues.

biomarkers.name: Red Blood Cell Count
biomarkers.description: Number of red blood cells carrying oxygen throughout the body.

biomarkers.name: Hemoglobin
biomarkers.description: Protein in red blood cells that carries oxygen. Low levels indicate anemia.

biomarkers.name: Hematocrit
biomarkers.description: Percentage of red blood cells in total blood volume.

biomarkers.name: Mean Corpuscular Volume
biomarkers.description: Average size of red blood cells. Helps classify type of anemia.

biomarkers.name: Mean Corpuscular Hemoglobin
biomarkers.description: Average amount of hemoglobin per red blood cell.

biomarkers.name: Mean Corpuscular Hemoglobin Concentration
biomarkers.description: Concentration of hemoglobin in a given volume of red blood cells.

biomarkers.name: Platelet Count
biomarkers.description: Cells that help blood clot. Low levels may cause bleeding; high levels may indicate clotting risk.

biomarkers.name: Neutrophils
biomarkers.description: Most abundant white blood cells; first responders to bacterial infection.

biomarkers.name: Lymphocytes
biomarkers.description: White blood cells key to immune response, including T and B cells.

biomarkers.name: Monocytes
biomarkers.description: White blood cells that fight infection and help other immune cells.

biomarkers.name: Eosinophils
biomarkers.description: White blood cells involved in allergic reactions and parasite defense.

biomarkers.name: Glucose
biomarkers.description: Blood sugar level. Elevated fasting glucose may indicate prediabetes or diabetes.

biomarkers.name: Blood Urea Nitrogen
biomarkers.description: Waste product from protein metabolism. Elevated levels may indicate kidney dysfunction.

biomarkers.name: Creatinine
biomarkers.description: Waste product filtered by kidneys. High levels suggest impaired kidney function.

biomarkers.name: Estimated GFR
biomarkers.description: Estimate of kidney filtration rate. Below 60 may indicate chronic kidney disease.

biomarkers.name: Sodium
biomarkers.description: Electrolyte that regulates fluid balance and nerve/muscle function.

biomarkers.name: Potassium
biomarkers.description: Electrolyte critical for heart and muscle function.

biomarkers.name: Chloride
biomarkers.description: Electrolyte that maintains fluid and acid-base balance.

biomarkers.name: Carbon Dioxide
biomarkers.description: Bicarbonate level reflecting acid-base balance.

biomarkers.name: Calcium
biomarkers.description: Mineral essential for bones, teeth, nerve, and muscle function.

biomarkers.name: Total Protein
biomarkers.description: Total amount of protein in the blood; reflects nutritional status and liver function.

biomarkers.name: Albumin
biomarkers.description: Main protein made by the liver. Low levels indicate liver disease or malnutrition.

biomarkers.name: Alanine Aminotransferase
biomarkers.description: Liver enzyme. Elevated levels indicate liver damage or inflammation.

biomarkers.name: Aspartate Aminotransferase
biomarkers.description: Enzyme found in the liver and heart. Elevated levels may indicate liver or heart damage.

biomarkers.name: Alkaline Phosphatase
biomarkers.description: Enzyme in the liver and bones. Elevated levels may indicate liver or bone disease.

biomarkers.name: Total Bilirubin
biomarkers.description: Breakdown product of red blood cells processed by the liver. High levels cause jaundice.

biomarkers.name: Direct Bilirubin
biomarkers.description: Water-soluble form of bilirubin processed by the liver.

biomarkers.name: Total Cholesterol
biomarkers.description: Total cholesterol in the blood. High levels increase cardiovascular disease risk.

biomarkers.name: HDL Cholesterol
biomarkers.description: 'Good' cholesterol. Higher levels are protective against heart disease.

biomarkers.name: LDL Cholesterol
biomarkers.description: 'Bad' cholesterol. High levels increase risk of heart disease and stroke.

biomarkers.name: Triglycerides
biomarkers.description: Type of fat in the blood. High levels increase risk of heart disease and pancreatitis.

biomarkers.name: Non-HDL Cholesterol
biomarkers.description: Total cholesterol minus HDL; includes all 'bad' cholesterol particles.

biomarkers.name: Thyroid Stimulating Hormone
biomarkers.description: Hormone that regulates thyroid function. Elevated TSH suggests hypothyroidism; low TSH suggests hyperthyroidism.

biomarkers.name: Free T4 (Thyroxine)
biomarkers.description: Active form of the main thyroid hormone.

biomarkers.name: Free T3 (Triiodothyronine)
biomarkers.description: The most active thyroid hormone, regulating metabolism.

biomarkers.name: Hemoglobin A1c
biomarkers.description: 3-month average blood sugar. Used to diagnose and monitor diabetes.

biomarkers.name: Fasting Insulin
biomarkers.description: Fasting insulin level; elevated levels may indicate insulin resistance.

biomarkers.name: Vitamin D (25-OH)
biomarkers.description: Essential for bone health, immune function, and mood regulation.

biomarkers.name: Vitamin B12
biomarkers.description: Essential for nerve function and red blood cell formation.

biomarkers.name: Folate
biomarkers.description: B vitamin important for DNA synthesis and cell division.

biomarkers.name: Ferritin
biomarkers.description: Iron storage protein. Low levels indicate iron deficiency; high may indicate inflammation.

biomarkers.name: Serum Iron
biomarkers.description: Amount of iron in the blood. Used alongside ferritin to evaluate iron status.

biomarkers.name: Total Iron Binding Capacity
biomarkers.description: Measure of the blood's capacity to bind iron. High in iron deficiency.

biomarkers.name: Magnesium
biomarkers.description: Mineral important for muscle, nerve, and heart function.

biomarkers.name: Zinc
biomarkers.description: Mineral essential for immune function, wound healing, and DNA synthesis.

biomarkers.name: Testosterone (Total)
biomarkers.description: Primary male sex hormone. Important for muscle, bone, mood, and libido.

biomarkers.name: Testosterone (Free)
biomarkers.description: Unbound testosterone available for use by the body.

biomarkers.name: Estradiol
biomarkers.description: Primary female sex hormone. Important for bone density and cardiovascular health in both sexes.

biomarkers.name: Cortisol (Morning)
biomarkers.description: Stress hormone produced by the adrenal glands. Regulates metabolism and immune response.

biomarkers.name: DHEA-S
biomarkers.description: Adrenal hormone that serves as a precursor to sex hormones.

biomarkers.name: IGF-1
biomarkers.description: Growth hormone marker. Used to evaluate growth hormone deficiency or excess.

biomarkers.name: Follicle Stimulating Hormone
biomarkers.description: Hormone regulating reproductive processes and fertility.

biomarkers.name: Luteinizing Hormone
biomarkers.description: Hormone that triggers ovulation in women and testosterone production in men.

biomarkers.name: C-Reactive Protein (hs-CRP)
biomarkers.description: Marker of systemic inflammation. Elevated levels increase cardiovascular disease risk.

biomarkers.name: Erythrocyte Sedimentation Rate
biomarkers.description: Non-specific marker of inflammation. Elevated in infections, autoimmune disease, and cancer.

biomarkers.name: Homocysteine
biomarkers.description: Amino acid linked to cardiovascular disease and vitamin B deficiencies when elevated.

biomarkers.name: Uric Acid
biomarkers.description: Breakdown product of purines. Elevated levels can cause gout and kidney stones.

biomarkers.name: Prostate-Specific Antigen
biomarkers.description: Protein produced by the prostate. Elevated levels may indicate prostate cancer or inflammation.
